name: Mercury
best_season_notes: Best near greatest elongation around dawn or dusk.
observation_notes: Low altitude often limits seeing and contrast.

name: Venus
observation_notes: Phase changes are easy to observe.

name: Mars
observation_notes: Surface detail depends strongly on seeing and angular size.

name: Jupiter
best_season_notes: Best near opposition and high culmination.
observation_notes: Excellent planetary target with belts and moon transits.

name: Saturn
observation_notes: Ring opening angle strongly affects appearance.

name: Uranus
best_season_notes: Best in dark skies near opposition.
observation_notes: Small cyan disk requires steady seeing.

name: Neptune
observation_notes: Faint small blue disk, challenging for small apertures.

name: Moon
best_season_notes: Visible through most of the month.
observation_notes: Bright target; use short exposures and note phase-dependent glare.

name: Sun
observation_notes: Never observe without proper solar filter.
